#beagle.string-builder

20/20 documented
new(capacity)
beagle.string-builder.bg:18

Construct an empty builder. capacity is a hint — the buffer grows automatically. Pass a large value if you know roughly how many bytes you'll write to skip intermediate growth.

append-byte!(sb, byte)
beagle.string-builder.bg:23

Append one byte (an Int 0..255). Out-of-range values are masked to a u8.

append-char!(sb, ch)
beagle.string-builder.bg:29

Append a single-character string (or any string really; the name just signals that this is a per-char hot path).

append!(sb, s)
beagle.string-builder.bg:34

Append a string. Works on flat strings, slices, and cons strings.

append-range!(sb, s, start, end)
beagle.string-builder.bg:39

Append bytes from s[start:end].

append-range-filter-byte!(sb, s, start, end, skip-byte)
beagle.string-builder.bg:44

Append bytes from s[start:end], skipping every occurrence of skip-byte.

append-range-uppercase!(sb, s, start, end)
beagle.string-builder.bg:49

Append bytes from s[start:end], converting ASCII letters to uppercase.

append-builder-range!(dst, src, start, end)
beagle.string-builder.bg:54

Append bytes from src[start:end] into dst.

index-byte(s, byte, start)
beagle.string-builder.bg:59

Return the byte index of byte in s at or after start, or -1.

append-int!(sb, i)
beagle.string-builder.bg:64

Append the decimal representation of an int.

append-float!(sb, f)
beagle.string-builder.bg:69

Append a float, formatted the same way println formats floats.

length(sb)
beagle.string-builder.bg:74

Number of bytes written so far.

capacity(sb)
beagle.string-builder.bg:79

Total backing-buffer capacity in bytes.

clear!(sb)
beagle.string-builder.bg:84

Reset length to 0. Keeps the backing buffer (no re-alloc).

byte-at(sb, i)
beagle.string-builder.bg:89

Read a byte by index (returns Int, or null if out of range).

set-byte-at!(sb, i, byte)
beagle.string-builder.bg:94

Overwrite the byte at index i. Returns sb. No-op if out of range.

reverse!(sb)
beagle.string-builder.bg:99

Reverse the bytes in place. ASCII-safe.

to-string(sb)
beagle.string-builder.bg:105

Materialize an immutable String containing the bytes appended so far. One allocation, one memcpy. Repeated calls produce independent strings.

append-line!(sb, s)
beagle.string-builder.bg:111

Append the string s followed by a newline ("\n"). Returns the builder so calls can be chained.

is-empty?(sb)
beagle.string-builder.bg:118

True when nothing has been appended yet (current byte length is 0).

#beagle.struct-pack

60/60 documented
require-unsigned(value, hi, code)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:32

Return value unchanged if it fits in unsigned [0, hi]; otherwise throw a range error naming the format code.

require-signed(value, lo, hi, code)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:43

Return value unchanged if it fits in signed [lo, hi]; otherwise throw a range error naming the format code.

mask-bits(value, bits)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:56

Mask value to its low bits bits (unsigned), yielding the two's-complement form of a negative value. Returns value unchanged when bits >= 62.

pack-u8(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:70

Pack an unsigned 8-bit value into a single-byte Vec<Int>.

pack-i8(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:75

Pack a signed 8-bit value (two's complement) into a single-byte Vec<Int>.

unpack-u8(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:80

Read an unsigned byte at offset.

unpack-i8(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:85

Read a signed byte (two's complement) at offset.

pack-u16-be(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:99

Pack an unsigned 16-bit value as big-endian (high byte first).

pack-u16-le(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:105

Pack an unsigned 16-bit value as little-endian (low byte first).

pack-u16(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:111

Pack an unsigned 16-bit value (big-endian default).

pack-i16-be(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:116

Pack a signed 16-bit value (two's complement) as big-endian.

pack-i16-le(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:121

Pack a signed 16-bit value (two's complement) as little-endian.

pack-i16(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:126

Pack a signed 16-bit value (big-endian default).

unpack-u16-be(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:131

Read an unsigned big-endian 16-bit value at offset.

unpack-u16-le(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:136

Read an unsigned little-endian 16-bit value at offset.

unpack-u16(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:141

Read an unsigned 16-bit value (big-endian default).

sign16(v)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:147

Sign-extend an unsigned 16-bit value v into a signed Int.

unpack-i16-be(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:156

Read a signed big-endian 16-bit value at offset.

unpack-i16-le(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:161

Read a signed little-endian 16-bit value at offset.

unpack-i16(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:166

Read a signed 16-bit value (big-endian default).

pack-u32-be(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:175

Pack an unsigned 32-bit value as big-endian (high byte first).

pack-u32-le(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:181

Pack an unsigned 32-bit value as little-endian (low byte first).

pack-u32(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:187

Pack an unsigned 32-bit value (big-endian default).

pack-i32-be(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:192

Pack a signed 32-bit value (two's complement) as big-endian.

pack-i32-le(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:197

Pack a signed 32-bit value (two's complement) as little-endian.

pack-i32(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:202

Pack a signed 32-bit value (big-endian default).

unpack-u32-be(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:207

Read an unsigned big-endian 32-bit value at offset.

unpack-u32-le(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:215

Read an unsigned little-endian 32-bit value at offset.

unpack-u32(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:223

Read an unsigned 32-bit value (big-endian default).

sign32(v)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:229

Sign-extend an unsigned 32-bit value v into a signed Int.

unpack-i32-be(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:238

Read a signed big-endian 32-bit value at offset.

unpack-i32-le(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:243

Read a signed little-endian 32-bit value at offset.

unpack-i32(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:248

Read a signed 32-bit value (big-endian default).

require-unsigned-64(value, code)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:267

Return value unchanged if it is non-negative (it then necessarily fits in unsigned 64 bits given Beagle's 62-bit ints); otherwise throw, naming code.

bytes-u64-be(v)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:279

Return the 8 big-endian bytes (high byte first) of v's low 64 bits as a Vec<Int>; negative v yields its two's-complement bytes.

bytes-u64-le(v)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:285

Return the 8 little-endian bytes (low byte first) of v's low 64 bits as a Vec<Int>; negative v yields its two's-complement bytes.

pack-u64-be(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:291

Pack an unsigned 64-bit value as big-endian (high byte first). Input must be non-negative (Beagle's 62-bit ints cannot express bit-63-set u64 values).

pack-u64-le(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:297

Pack an unsigned 64-bit value as little-endian (low byte first). Input must be non-negative.

pack-u64(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:302

Pack an unsigned 64-bit value (big-endian default).

pack-i64-be(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:309

Pack a signed 64-bit value (two's complement) as big-endian.

pack-i64-le(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:314

Pack a signed 64-bit value (two's complement) as little-endian.

pack-i64(value)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:319

Pack a signed 64-bit value (big-endian default).

unpack-u64-be(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:325

Read an unsigned big-endian 64-bit value at offset. Only round-trips values within Beagle's 62-bit int range (larger u64s are unrepresentable).

unpack-u64-le(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:338

Read an unsigned little-endian 64-bit value at offset. Only round-trips values within Beagle's 62-bit int range.

unpack-u64(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:350

Read an unsigned 64-bit value (big-endian default).

unpack-i64-be(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:357

Read a signed big-endian 64-bit value at offset. Sign-extends from the most-significant byte, so negatives round-trip without forming the unrepresentable unsigned 64-bit value.

unpack-i64-le(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:369

Read a signed little-endian 64-bit value at offset. Sign-extends from the most-significant (last) byte so negatives round-trip correctly.

unpack-i64(bytes, offset)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:380

Read a signed 64-bit value (big-endian default).

append-all(dst, src)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:404

Append every element of src onto dst, returning the resulting Vec<Int>.

order-is-le(c)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:421

True when order char c selects little-endian (< or =); false otherwise.

code-width(c)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:428

Return the byte width of field code c (1 for b/B/x, 2 for h/H, 4 for i/I, 8 for q/Q); throws on an unknown code.

pack-one(c, value, le)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:451

Pack a single value for field code c, little-endian when le is true, returning its Vec<Int> bytes; throws on an unknown code.

unpack-one(c, bytes, offset, le)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:490

Read a single value for field code c from bytes at offset, little-endian when le is true; throws on an unknown code.

is-order-char(c)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:528

True when c is an endianness/order marker (>, <, !, or =).

is-skip-char(c)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:535

True when c is whitespace (space, tab, newline, or carriage return) to be ignored within a format string.

format-body-start(format)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:543

Return the index where field codes begin in format: 1 when a leading order char is present, otherwise 0.

format-is-le(format)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:554

True when format's optional leading order char selects little-endian; defaults to false (big-endian) when no order char is present.

pack(format, values)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:563

Pack a vector of values according to format, returning a Vec<Int> of bytes. Each value-bearing field code consumes one value in order; the pad code x writes a zero byte and consumes no value.

pack(">HHB", [0x0102, 0x0304, 0x05]) == [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

unpack(format, bytes)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:595

Unpack bytes according to format, returning a Vec<Int> of values in field order. The pad code x skips a byte and produces no value.

unpack(">HHB", [1,2,3,4,5]) == [258, 772, 5]

calcsize(format)
beagle.struct-pack.bg:628

Compute the total byte size of a format string (like Python's calcsize).

#beagle.template

25/25 documented
ws-code?(c)
beagle.template.bg:78

True if char-code c is an ASCII whitespace character (space, tab, newline, CR, form-feed, or vertical-tab).

strip(s)
beagle.template.bg:86

Trim ASCII whitespace from both ends of string s and return the substring. Self-contained (does not rely on core/trim).

matches-at?(s, i, pat)
beagle.template.bg:101

True if the literal pat occurs in s starting exactly at index i.

find-from(s, pat, start)
beagle.template.bg:114

Return the first index >= start where pat occurs in s, or -1 if not found.

tokenize(src)
beagle.template.bg:139

Scan src left-to-right into a vector of Tokens, splitting on the {{{ }}}, {{ }}, and {% %} delimiters (triple-brace is tested first). Throws a clear error on an unterminated block.

flush-literal(tokens, src, start, end)
beagle.template.bg:194

Append a "literal" Token for src[start:end] to tokens if the range is non-empty; otherwise return tokens unchanged.

parse-path(expr)
beagle.template.bg:211

Split a dotted path string like "user.name" into a vector of keyword segments [:user :name]. Throws on an empty or malformed path (e.g. "a..b" or a leading/trailing dot).

parse-expr(text, default-escape)
beagle.template.bg:237

Parse the inside of an interpolation into a VarNode. An optional " | raw" filter forces escape false; default-escape sets the base (true for {{ }}, false for {{{ }}}). Throws on any unknown filter.

tag-keyword(tag)
beagle.template.bg:255

Return the first whitespace-delimited word of a tag body, e.g. "if x" -> "if".

tag-rest(tag)
beagle.template.bg:267

Return everything after the first word of a tag body, stripped, e.g. "for x in items" -> "x in items".

parse-body(tokens, start, stops)
beagle.template.bg:291

Recursive-descent parse of tokens from index start, building a node vector until it hits a tag whose keyword is in stops (which it does NOT consume). Returns [nodes, next-index].

is-stop?(kw, stops)
beagle.template.bg:339

True if tag keyword kw is one of the terminating keywords in the stops vector.

parse-if(tokens, start)
beagle.template.bg:357

Parse an {% if %} block beginning at tokens[start], consuming an optional {% else %} and the closing {% endif %}. Returns [IfNode, next-index]. Throws if the condition is empty or the block is unterminated.

parse-for(tokens, start)
beagle.template.bg:394

Parse a {% for VAR in ITEMS %} block beginning at tokens[start], consuming the closing {% endfor %}. Returns [ForNode, next-index]. Throws on malformed syntax or a missing {% endfor %}.

parse(tokens)
beagle.template.bg:424

Parse the full token vector into a top-level node vector. Throws if a stray endif/else/endfor tag is found with no open block.

lookup(ctx, path)
beagle.template.bg:445

Resolve a keyword path against context map ctx via chained get, trying the keyword key first then falling back to its string form. Returns null if any segment is missing.

truthy?(v)
beagle.template.bg:473

Jinja-style truthiness for {% if %}: null, false, the empty string, the empty vector, and numeric zero (Int or Float) are falsy; everything else is truthy.

html-escape(s)
beagle.template.bg:504

Return s with the five significant HTML characters (& < > " ') replaced by their entities, processed character by character.

render-value(v)
beagle.template.bg:540

Render a value to its string form for output: null becomes "" (so missing vars vanish), strings pass through, and everything else goes through to-string.

render-body(builder, nodes, ctx)
beagle.template.bg:555

Render each node in nodes into the string-builder against context ctx. Returns null.

render-node(builder, node, ctx)
beagle.template.bg:567

Render a single AST node (TextNode/VarNode/IfNode/ForNode) into builder against context ctx. Throws on an unknown node type.

render-for(builder, node, ctx)
beagle.template.bg:601

Render a ForNode by iterating its vector and rendering the body once per element with the loop var bound in a child context. A null collection renders nothing; a non-vector value throws a clear error.

compile(template)
beagle.template.bg:638

Tokenize and parse template ONCE into a reusable CompiledTemplate. Render it against many contexts with render-compiled to avoid re-parsing the same string repeatedly. Throws on malformed template syntax (the same errors render raises).

render-compiled(compiled, ctx)
beagle.template.bg:648

Render an already-compiled template against context map ctx. Equivalent to render but skips the tokenize+parse step, so it is cheap to call repeatedly with the same compiled template. Throws if compiled is not a CompiledTemplate.

render(template, ctx)
beagle.template.bg:670

Render a template string against a context map.

Context keys are keywords; template variable paths are dotted strings resolved as nested keyword lookups. Interpolated values are HTML-escaped unless the | raw filter or triple-brace {{{ }}} is used.

Equivalent to render-compiled(compile(template), ctx); use compile + render-compiled directly when rendering one template many times.

Examples: render("Hello {{ name }}!", {:name "World"}) // => "Hello World!" render("{{ user.name }}", {:user {:name "Ann"}}) // => "Ann"

#beagle.test

11/11 documented
new-results()
beagle.test.bg:25

Create a fresh, empty results accumulator (an atom).

results-snapshot(acc)
beagle.test.bg:30

Snapshot the current results map out of the accumulator atom.

record-pass(acc)
beagle.test.bg:36

Increment the :passed count in the accumulator atom acc. Returns null.

record-fail(acc, failure)
beagle.test.bg:47

Increment the :failed count and append failure (a map describing what went wrong) to the :failures list in accumulator atom acc. Returns null.

assert-eq(acc, actual, expected, name)
beagle.test.bg:57

Assert that actual equals expected (structural equality). Records a pass or a failure into acc. Returns true on pass.

assert-true(acc, cond, name)
beagle.test.bg:73

Assert that cond is truthy (not null, not false). Records a pass or a failure into acc. Returns true on pass.

assert-throws(acc, thunk, name)
beagle.test.bg:89

Assert that calling thunk (a zero-arg function) throws. Passes if the thunk raises; fails if it returns normally. Returns true on pass.

print-failures(failures, idx, len)
beagle.test.bg:112

Recursively print each failure in failures from index idx up to len, one FAIL [name] (kind): message line per failure. Returns null.

print-summary(results)
beagle.test.bg:123

Print a human-readable summary of a results map.

run-tests-loop(test-fns, idx, len, acc)
beagle.test.bg:145

Recursively invoke each test function in test-fns from index idx up to len, passing accumulator acc. A test that throws is caught and recorded as a "test-crash" failure rather than aborting the run. Returns null.

run-tests(test-fns)
beagle.test.bg:170

Run a vector of one-arg test functions fn(acc) { ... }. Each test function calls the assert helpers (assert-eq / assert-true / assert-throws) with the accumulator it is given. A test function that itself throws is caught and recorded as a failure (so one broken test never aborts the run).

Returns {:passed n :failed n :failures [..]} and prints a summary.

#beagle.test-async

12/16 documented
default-watchdog-ms()
beagle.test-async.bg:46

Default watchdog budget in milliseconds (30000) used by every assertion; tunable per call.

deadline-after(watchdog_ms)
beagle.test-async.bg:51

Convert a millisecond watchdog budget into an absolute monotonic-nanosecond deadline.

past-deadline?(deadline)
beagle.test-async.bg:56

True once the monotonic clock has passed the given nanosecond deadline.

spin-burn(n)
beagle.test-async.bg:64

Burn n iterations of pure CPU (no allocation, no GC) to back off between empty polls; returns null.

receive-within(ch, watchdog_ms, what)
beagle.test-async.bg:81

Busy-spin receive one value from ch, returning it as soon as present; throws (hard fail) naming what if the watchdog fires first.

receive-within-spin(ch, deadline, watchdog_ms, what, backoff)
beagle.test-async.bg:86

Recursive spin helper for receive-within: polls try-receive with growing backoff until a value arrives, else throws once past deadline.

count-equal(coll, x)
beagle.test-async.bg:107

Count elements of coll structurally equal to x.

multiset-eq?(a, b)
beagle.test-async.bg:123

True iff a and b are equal as multisets (same elements with the same multiplicities, order irrelevant).

assert-set-eq!(actual, expected)
beagle.test-async.bg:141

Assert actual and expected are equal as multisets (order-insensitive); throws on mismatch, returns null.

collect-n(ch, n, watchdog_ms)
beagle.test-async.bg:171

Receive exactly n values from ch (each watchdog-bounded), returning them in arrival order.

assert-channel-drained!(ch)
beagle.test-async.bg:214

Assert ch holds no unreceived values (catches over-production); throws if any remain. Call only after a completion barrier proves all producers are done.

await-condition-spin!(pred, deadline, watchdog_ms, backoff)
beagle.test-async.bg:239

Recursive spin helper for await-condition!: polls pred with growing backoff until truthy, else throws once past deadline.

4 undocumented
  • assert-receives!(ch, expected)assert-receives!(ch, expected, watchdog_ms)arities 2, 3
  • assert-receives-all!(ch, n, expected-set)assert-receives-all!(ch, n, expected-set, watchdog_ms)arities 3, 4
  • await-condition!(pred)await-condition!(pred, watchdog_ms)arities 1, 2
  • assert-eventually!(pred)assert-eventually!(pred, watchdog_ms)arities 1, 2

#beagle.text

30/33 documented
code-at(s, i)
beagle.text.bg:25

Return the char-code (codepoint) of the character at index i in s. Thin wrapper over char-code(s[i]).

ws-code?(c)
beagle.text.bg:32

True if code point c is ASCII whitespace (space, tab, newline, carriage-return, form-feed, or vertical-tab).

digit-code?(c)
beagle.text.bg:39

True if code point c is an ASCII decimal digit ('0'..'9').

repeat-string(s, n)
beagle.text.bg:54

Repeat a string n times, concatenated together.

Returns "" when n <= 0.

Examples: repeat-string("ab", 3) // => "ababab" repeat-string("x", 0) // => ""

clip-fill(pad, n)
beagle.text.bg:126

Build a fill string of exactly n characters by repeating pad and clipping the result to length n. Helper for pad-left/pad-right.

chars(s)
beagle.text.bg:159

Return the characters of s as a vector of single-character strings.

Examples: chars("abc") // => ["a", "b", "c"]

lines(s)
beagle.text.bg:175

Split s into a vector of lines on "\n" (a trailing newline yields a final empty string). Carriage returns are left intact.

Examples: lines("a\nb\nc") // => ["a", "b", "c"]

capitalize(s)
beagle.text.bg:188

Uppercase the first character, lowercase the rest.

Examples: capitalize("hELLO") // => "Hello" capitalize("") // => ""

title-case(s)
beagle.text.bg:203

Capitalize the first letter of each whitespace-separated word.

Collapses runs of whitespace to a single space in the output.

Examples: title-case("the quick brown fox") // => "The Quick Brown Fox" title-case(" hELLO wORLD ") // => "Hello World"

char-set-contains?(chars, ch)
beagle.text.bg:225

True if the single-character string ch appears in chars (treated as a set of characters). Thin wrapper over index-of.

trim-chars(s, chars)
beagle.text.bg:238

Trim every leading and trailing character that appears in chars.

chars is a string treated as a set of characters to strip. Unlike core's trim (whitespace only), this strips an arbitrary set.

Examples: trim-chars("xxhelloxx", "x") // => "hello" trim-chars("[[data]]", "[]") // => "data" trim-chars("--a-b--", "-") // => "a-b"

trim-chars-left(s, chars)
beagle.text.bg:252

Trim only leading characters in chars.

trim-chars-right(s, chars)
beagle.text.bg:262

Trim only trailing characters in chars.

replace-all(s, old, new)
beagle.text.bg:284

Replace every non-overlapping occurrence of old with new.

Matches core's replace semantics (replaces ALL occurrences). Provided here under the conventional name. Throws on an empty old (which would otherwise loop forever).

Examples: replace-all("a.b.c", ".", "/") // => "a/b/c" replace-all("aaa", "a", "bb") // => "bbbbbb"

replace-first(s, old, new)
beagle.text.bg:320

Replace only the FIRST occurrence of old with new.

If old does not occur in s, returns s unchanged. Companion to replace-all. Throws on an empty old (an empty needle has no well-defined single occurrence).

Examples: replace-first("a.b.c", ".", "/") // => "a/b.c" replace-first("hello", "l", "L") // => "heLlo" replace-first("abc", "x", "y") // => "abc"

split-with-limit(s, delim, limit)
beagle.text.bg:347

Split s on delim, producing at most limit pieces.

The final piece holds the unsplit remainder (delimiters and all). A limit <= 0 means "no limit" (split fully). Returns a vector of strings.

Examples: split-with-limit("a,b,c,d", ",", 2) // => ["a", "b,c,d"] split-with-limit("a,b,c,d", ",", 3) // => ["a", "b", "c,d"] split-with-limit("a,b,c", ",", 0) // => ["a", "b", "c"] split-with-limit("abc", ",", 2) // => ["abc"]

starts-with-any?(s, prefixes)
beagle.text.bg:388

True if s starts with ANY of the given prefix strings.

prefixes is any seqable collection of strings.

Examples: starts-with-any?("http://x", ["http://", "https://"]) // => true starts-with-any?("ftp://x", ["http://", "https://"]) // => false

ends-with-any?(s, suffixes)
beagle.text.bg:396

True if s ends with ANY of the given suffix strings.

Examples: ends-with-any?("a.png", [".png", ".jpg"]) // => true

contains-any?(s, subs)
beagle.text.bg:405

True if s contains ANY of the given substrings.

Examples: contains-any?("hello world", ["xyz", "wor"]) // => true contains-any?("hello", ["a", "b"]) // => false

parse-float(s)
beagle.text.bg:427

Parse a string into a Float. Returns null on any malformed input.

Accepts an optional leading sign, an integer part, an optional fractional part, and an optional exponent (e/E with optional sign). At least one digit must be present overall.

Examples: parse-float("3.14") // => 3.14 parse-float("-0.5") // => -0.5 parse-float("10") // => 10.0 parse-float("1e3") // => 1000.0 parse-float("2.5e-2") // => 0.025 parse-float("abc") // => null parse-float("") // => null

hex-digit(d, upper)
beagle.text.bg:522

Return the hex digit character for value d (0..15) as a single-char string. When upper is true, letters are uppercase ('A'..'F'), otherwise lowercase.

int->hex(n, upper)
beagle.text.bg:542

Render a non-negative int as a hexadecimal string.

Negative inputs are rendered with a leading '-'.

Examples: int->hex(255, false) // => "ff" int->hex(255, true) // => "FF" int->hex(0, false) // => "0"

fixed(x, decimals)
beagle.text.bg:578

Render a number with EXACTLY decimals fractional digits, rounded HALF-UP.

Sign is split off and reapplied; the fraction is zero-padded on the left to exactly decimals digits; rounding that reaches a whole unit carries into the integer part (e.g. 9.999 -> "10.00"). Works on ints and floats.

Examples: fixed(3.14159, 2) // => "3.14" fixed(3.14159, 6) // => "3.141590" fixed(9.999, 2) // => "10.00" fixed(0.0 - 9.999, 2) // => "-10.00" fixed(3.0, 0) // => "3"

round-to(x, n)
beagle.text.bg:623

Round a number to n decimal places, returning a NUMBER (not a string).

Use this when you need the rounded value for further math; use fixed when you want a string with a guaranteed number of decimals.

Examples: round-to(3.14159, 2) // => 3.14 round-to(2.5, 0) // => 3.0

commas(n)
beagle.text.bg:639

Insert thousands separators into an integer, returning a string.

Examples: commas(1234567) // => "1,234,567" commas(-1000) // => "-1,000" commas(42) // => "42"

count-occurrences(s, sub)
beagle.text.bg:670

Count non-overlapping occurrences of sub in s.

Examples: count-occurrences("banana", "a") // => 3 count-occurrences("aaaa", "aa") // => 2

reverse-string(s)
beagle.text.bg:694

Reverse the characters of a string.

Examples: reverse-string("abc") // => "cba"

string->bytes(s)
beagle.text.bg:724

Return the UTF-8 byte values (each 0..255) of a string as a Vec<Int>.

Multi-byte codepoints expand to their full UTF-8 encoding — e.g. "é" is [195, 169], not [233].

Examples: string->bytes("A") // => [65] string->bytes("é") // => [195, 169] string->bytes("") // => []

bytes->string(vec)
beagle.text.bg:745

Build a String from a Vec of UTF-8 byte values (each an Int 0..255).

Inverse of string->bytes. The bytes must form valid UTF-8.

Examples: bytes->string([65]) // => "A" bytes->string([195, 169]) // => "é" bytes->string([]) // => ""

ellipsize(s, n, ellipsis)
beagle.text.bg:764

Truncate s to at most n characters, appending ellipsis if truncated.

The result (including the ellipsis) is at most n characters. If n is smaller than the ellipsis, the ellipsis itself is truncated.

Examples: ellipsize("hello world", 8, "...") // => "hello..." ellipsize("hi", 8, "...") // => "hi"

3 undocumented
  • pad-left(s, width)pad-left(s, width, pad)arities 2, 3
  • pad-right(s, width)pad-right(s, width, pad)arities 2, 3
  • center(s, width)center(s, width, pad)arities 2, 3

#beagle.textwrap

13/14 documented
code-at(s, i)
beagle.textwrap.bg:31

Return the char-code (code point) of the character at index i in s.

ws-code?(c)
beagle.textwrap.bg:37

True if code point c is ASCII whitespace (space, tab, LF, CR, FF, or VT).

ws-char?(ch)
beagle.textwrap.bg:43

True if the single-character string ch is ASCII whitespace.

split-words(text)
beagle.textwrap.bg:57

Split text into whitespace-delimited words. Whitespace runs are collapsed and leading/trailing whitespace is dropped. Returns a vector of non-empty word strings (empty vector for empty/whitespace-only input).

wrap(text, width)
beagle.textwrap.bg:98

Greedy word-wrap text to lines of at most width characters.

Words are kept whole and never split. A line packs as many words as fit (joined with single spaces). A single word longer than width gets its own line and is allowed to overflow (it is NOT broken). Leading/trailing whitespace is stripped and internal whitespace runs are collapsed.

Returns a vector of line strings (empty vector for empty/whitespace-only input). Throws on width <= 0.

Examples: wrap("the quick brown fox", 9) // => ["the quick", "brown fox"] wrap("supercalifragilistic", 9) // => ["supercalifragilistic"] wrap("", 9) // => []

fill(text, width)
beagle.textwrap.bg:145

Wrap text to width and join the resulting lines with newlines.

Equivalent to join(wrap(text, width), "\n"). Returns "" for empty input.

Examples: fill("the quick brown fox", 9) // => "the quick\nbrown fox"

split-lines-keep(text)
beagle.textwrap.bg:159

Split text into lines on "\n", preserving empty lines and any trailing empty segment (so "a\n" => ["a", ""]). Returns a vector of line strings.

leading-ws-len(line)
beagle.textwrap.bg:177

Return the number of leading whitespace characters at the start of line.

blank-line?(line)
beagle.textwrap.bg:188

True if line is empty or consists entirely of whitespace.

common-prefix-len(a, b)
beagle.textwrap.bg:196

Return the length of the longest common prefix of strings a and b, compared character by character.

dedent(text)
beagle.textwrap.bg:220

Remove the longest common leading-whitespace prefix from every line.

Lines that consist solely of whitespace are ignored when computing the common prefix (matching Python's textwrap.dedent) and are normalized to empty lines in the output. Mixed tabs/spaces are compared literally.

Examples: dedent(" a\n b") // => "a\nb" dedent(" a\n b\n c") // => "a\n b\nc" dedent("") // => ""

indent(text, prefix)
beagle.textwrap.bg:274

Add prefix to the start of every non-empty line in text.

Lines that are empty or whitespace-only are left untouched (matching Python's textwrap.indent default predicate). A trailing newline in the input is preserved.

Examples: indent("a\nb", "> ") // => "> a\n> b" indent("a\n\nb", "> ") // => "> a\n\n> b" indent("a\nb\n", "> ") // => "> a\n> b\n"

trim-left-ws(s)
beagle.textwrap.bg:377

Return s with any leading whitespace removed (trailing whitespace kept).

1 undocumented
  • shorten(text, width)shorten(text, width, placeholder)arities 2, 3

#beagle.time

17/17 documented
libc-path()
beagle.time.bg:24

Returns the platform-specific libc shared-library to load via FFI: libSystem on macOS, the glibc .so on Linux, falling back to "libc.so.6".

epoch-seconds()
beagle.time.bg:56

Returns the current Unix time in whole seconds (UTC) via libc time(NULL).

epoch-millis()
beagle.time.bg:64

Returns the current Unix time in milliseconds (UTC), reading a struct timeval via libc gettimeofday and combining tv_sec*1000 + tv_usec/1000.

floor-div(a, b)
beagle.time.bg:85

Euclidean/floored integer division (rounds toward negative infinity), unlike Beagle's / which truncates toward zero. Needed so the calendar math stays correct for negative (pre-1970) inputs.

floor-mod(a, b)
beagle.time.bg:103

Floored modulo paired with floor-div: the result has the same sign as the divisor b. Thin wrapper computing a - floor-div(a, b) * b.

days-from-civil(y, m, d)
beagle.time.bg:116

Returns the number of days from 1970-01-01 to the civil date y-m-d (month 1..12, day 1..31), negative for pre-epoch dates. Uses Hinnant's proleptic-Gregorian algorithm.

civil-from-days(z0)
beagle.time.bg:131

Inverse of days-from-civil: converts a day count since 1970-01-01 (z0, may be negative) into a [year, month, day] civil date.

days-since-epoch(epoch)
beagle.time.bg:151

Returns the floored count of whole days since 1970-01-01 for an epoch-seconds value epoch.

weekday(epoch)
beagle.time.bg:159

Returns the weekday for an epoch-seconds value epoch, where 0 = Sunday .. 6 = Saturday.

civil-from-epoch(epoch)
beagle.time.bg:168

Decomposes an epoch-seconds value epoch into the UTC tuple [year, month, day, hour, minute, second, weekday].

append-2!(b, n)
beagle.time.bg:189

Appends n to string-builder b as a zero-padded two-digit decimal.

append-4!(b, n)
beagle.time.bg:202

Appends n to string-builder b as a zero-padded four-digit decimal (assumes n >= 0).

http-date(epoch)
beagle.time.bg:213

Formats an epoch-seconds value epoch as an RFC 1123 / HTTP Date string in GMT, e.g. "Sun, 06 Nov 1994 08:49:37 GMT".

ms-of-seconds(s)
beagle.time.bg:252

Converts a whole-second count s into milliseconds (multiplies by 1000). Use this to make the unit explicit when building a millisecond duration.

ms-of-minutes(m)
beagle.time.bg:258

Converts a whole-minute count m into milliseconds (multiplies by 60000). Use this to make the unit explicit when building a millisecond duration.

ms-of-hours(h)
beagle.time.bg:264

Converts a whole-hour count h into milliseconds (multiplies by 3600000). Use this to make the unit explicit when building a millisecond duration.

format-duration(ms)
beagle.time.bg:275

Formats a non-negative millisecond count ms as a compact human-readable duration string. The output concatenates the non-zero hour/minute/second/ millisecond components with single-letter units (h, m, s, ms), omitting any component that is zero. Examples: 0 -> "0ms", 500 -> "500ms", 1500 -> "1s500ms", 3661000 -> "1h1m1s". A ms of 0 yields the literal "0ms". Throws on a negative input (durations are non-negative; use a sign elsewhere if you need a delta).

#beagle.tls

6/6 documented
connect(host, port)
beagle.tls.bg:19

Open a TLS connection to host:port, returning an opaque connection handle. Throws if the TCP connect, TLS handshake, or certificate verification fails.

Examples: let c = connect("example.com", 443)

write(conn, data)
beagle.tls.bg:30

Write data (a string, byte-faithful) over the TLS connection. Returns the number of bytes written. Throws on a write error.

read(conn, n)
beagle.tls.bg:41

Read up to n bytes of plaintext from the TLS connection, returning them as a byte-faithful string. Returns "" at end-of-stream.

read-all(conn)
beagle.tls.bg:47

Read everything until end-of-stream, returning the full body as a string. (Suitable for HTTP responses sent with Connection: close.)

read-all-into(conn, builder)
beagle.tls.bg:54

Internal: append successive reads into builder until end-of-stream.

close(conn)
beagle.tls.bg:65

Close the TLS connection (sends a close_notify and releases the handle).

#beagle.url

20/20 documented
hex-digit(n)
beagle.url.bg:25

Map a nibble value 0..15 to its uppercase hex character ("0".."9", "A".."F"). Throws if n is outside 0..15 so codec bugs surface immediately.

hex-value(code)
beagle.url.bg:41

Convert a hex-digit char code into its value 0..15 (upper or lower case), or -1 if code is not a hex digit.

unreserved-byte?(b)
beagle.url.bg:60

True if byte b is in the RFC 3986 unreserved set (A-Z a-z 0-9 - _ . ~), i.e. a byte that need not be percent-encoded.

encode-char-into(result, ch)
beagle.url.bg:78

Percent-encode a single character ch into the result byte-builder: unreserved bytes are copied verbatim, all others emitted as %XX (uppercase hex) over the character's UTF-8 bytes. Returns null.

encode-component(s)
beagle.url.bg:107

Percent-encode string s as a URI component: every byte outside the unreserved set A-Za-z0-9-_.~ becomes %XX (uppercase hex). Returns a new string.

decode-component(s)
beagle.url.bg:127

Decode a percent-encoded component string: %XX -> byte, '+' kept literal, malformed '%' left verbatim. For form decoding (where '+' is a space) use decode-form/parse-query instead. Returns a new string.

decode-form(s)
beagle.url.bg:135

Decode s with application/x-www-form-urlencoded semantics: '+' becomes a space and %XX becomes its byte. Returns a new string.

decode-into(s, plus-is-space)
beagle.url.bg:145

Shared decode driver for decode-component/decode-form. When plus-is-space is true, '+' decodes to a space; otherwise it stays literal. Decoded bytes are reassembled as UTF-8. Returns a new string.

append-char-bytes(result, ch)
beagle.url.bg:184

Append the raw UTF-8 bytes of a single character ch onto the result byte-builder. Returns null.

parse-query(s)
beagle.url.bg:211

Parse an x-www-form-urlencoded query string into a map of string keys to string values (both form-decoded). A leading '?' is stripped, empty pairs from "&&" are skipped, a key without '=' maps to "", and on duplicate keys the last value wins.

parse-pair-into(m, pair)
beagle.url.bg:239

Form-decode one "key=value" pair (or bare "key", which yields "") and assoc it into map m, returning the updated map.

index-of-char(s, code)
beagle.url.bg:253

Return the index of the first character in s whose char-code equals code, or -1 if none is found.

build-query(m)
beagle.url.bg:275

Build an x-www-form-urlencoded query string from map m. Keys and values are coerced to strings and percent-encoded with component rules (space -> %20), joined by '&' in the map's key-iteration order. Round-trips through parse-query.

coerce-string(x)
beagle.url.bg:298

Coerce a query key/value to a string: keywords become their name, existing strings pass through, everything else goes through to-string.

parse-query-multi(s)
beagle.url.bg:322

Parse an x-www-form-urlencoded query string into a map of string keys to vectors of string values, preserving duplicate keys in encounter order (the complement of parse-query, which is last-wins). A leading '?' is stripped, empty pairs from "&&" are skipped, and a bare key (no '=') yields a single empty-string value. "a=1&a=2" => {"a" ["1", "2"]}.

parse-pair-multi-into(m, pair)
beagle.url.bg:350

Form-decode one "key=value" pair (or bare "key", which yields "") and append its value onto the existing vector for that key in map m, creating a new one-element vector for a first-seen key. Returns the updated map.

last-index-of-char(s, code)
beagle.url.bg:393

Return the index of the last character in s whose char-code equals code, or -1 if none is found.

all-digits?(s)
beagle.url.bg:408

True when s is non-empty and every character is an ASCII digit 0-9.

parse-url(s)
beagle.url.bg:437

Parse an absolute URL "scheme://host[:port][/path][?query][#fragment]" into a Url struct. Throws if the "://" separator or the host is missing. port is null unless a trailing all-digit port is present; query/fragment are the raw strings (null when their delimiter is absent); path is "" when none is given.

format-url(u)
beagle.url.bg:535

Reconstruct a URL string from a Url struct (the inverse of parse-url). Emits ":port" only when port is non-null, "?query" only when query is non-null, and "#fragment" only when fragment is non-null; an empty-string query or fragment still re-emits its leading '?' or '#'.

#beagle.ws

35/35 documented
opcode-continuation()
beagle.ws.bg:34

RFC 6455 opcode value for a continuation frame (0x0).

opcode-text()
beagle.ws.bg:36

RFC 6455 opcode value for a text frame (0x1).

opcode-binary()
beagle.ws.bg:38

RFC 6455 opcode value for a binary frame (0x2).

opcode-close()
beagle.ws.bg:40

RFC 6455 opcode value for a close control frame (0x8).

opcode-ping()
beagle.ws.bg:42

RFC 6455 opcode value for a ping control frame (0x9).

opcode-pong()
beagle.ws.bg:44

RFC 6455 opcode value for a pong control frame (0xA).

to-buf(s)
beagle.ws.bg:61

Wrap a byte-faithful string in a string-builder so its raw bytes can be read by index regardless of UTF-8 validity.

length-or-zero(s)
beagle.ws.bg:71

Return length(s), or 0 if length() throws; used only as a builder size hint for possibly-invalid-UTF-8 wire strings.

buf-byte(buf, i)
beagle.ws.bg:81

Read byte i (0..255) from a builder produced by to-buf.

hex-to-bytes(hex)
beagle.ws.bg:89

Convert a lowercase hex string into a Vec<Int> of byte values, one byte per pair of hex digits.

hex-digit-value(code)
beagle.ws.bg:104

Map a single hex-digit char-code (0-9, a-f, A-F) to its 0..15 value; throws on any non-hex code.

accept-key(client-key)
beagle.ws.bg:130

Compute the Sec-WebSocket-Accept value (base64(sha1(key ++ GUID))) for a client's Sec-WebSocket-Key.

is-websocket-upgrade?(request)
beagle.ws.bg:142

True if the http/Request is a valid RFC 6455 upgrade: Upgrade: websocket, a Connection header containing "upgrade", and a Sec-WebSocket-Key present.

handshake-response(request)
beagle.ws.bg:161

Build the 101 Switching Protocols http/Response that completes the handshake; throws if the request has no Sec-WebSocket-Key header.

parse-frame(bytes)
beagle.ws.bg:205

Parse one complete WebSocket frame from a byte-faithful string starting at index 0, handling 126/127 extended lengths and XOR-unmasking; returns a Frame.

encode-frame(opcode, payload)
beagle.ws.bg:290

Encode an unmasked, FIN=1 server->client frame for the given opcode and payload String; returns a byte-faithful String suitable for socket/write.

text-frame(payload)
beagle.ws.bg:325

Encode payload as a text frame (thin encode-frame wrapper).

binary-frame(payload)
beagle.ws.bg:330

Encode payload as a binary frame (thin encode-frame wrapper).

ping-frame(payload)
beagle.ws.bg:335

Encode payload as a ping control frame (thin encode-frame wrapper).

pong-frame(payload)
beagle.ws.bg:340

Encode payload as a pong control frame (thin encode-frame wrapper).

close-frame()
beagle.ws.bg:347

Encode an empty (codeless, clean) close frame.

text?(frame)
beagle.ws.bg:353

True if the parsed frame is a text frame.

binary?(frame)
beagle.ws.bg:355

True if the parsed frame is a binary frame.

ping?(frame)
beagle.ws.bg:357

True if the parsed frame is a ping frame.

pong?(frame)
beagle.ws.bg:359

True if the parsed frame is a pong frame.

close?(frame)
beagle.ws.bg:361

True if the parsed frame is a close frame.

continuation?(frame)
beagle.ws.bg:363

True if the parsed frame is a continuation frame (opcode 0x0).

frag-step(cur-opcode, cur-payload, frame)
beagle.ws.bg:380

Pure fragmentation step: fold one data/continuation frame into the current (opcode, payload) state, emitting a completed {:opcode, :payload} on a fin frame.

reassemble(frames)
beagle.ws.bg:401

Reassemble a list of data/continuation frames into complete messages ({:opcode, :payload}); single (fin=true) frames pass through as one message each.

reassemble-helper(frames, i, cur-opcode, cur-payload, out)
beagle.ws.bg:406

Internal fold for reassemble.

read-exact-buf(conn, n)
beagle.ws.bg:427

Read up to n bytes from conn into a byte-faithful string-builder, looping over short socket reads and stopping at EOF.

read-frame(conn)
beagle.ws.bg:453

Read and parse one complete WebSocket frame off the socket; returns a Frame, or null at EOF (connection closed before a full header arrived).

do-handshake(conn)
beagle.ws.bg:506

Perform the HTTP upgrade handshake on conn: on a valid WebSocket upgrade, write the 101 response and return true; otherwise write a 400 and return false.

serve-ws(host, port, on-message)
beagle.ws.bg:532

Cooperative single-thread WebSocket server: accept connections one at a time, upgrade each, and run its frame loop via on-message. GC-safe; blocks forever.

handle-ws-connection(conn, on-message)
beagle.ws.bg:553

Run one upgraded connection's frame loop (text/binary -> on-message reply, ping -> pong, close -> close), isolated in a try so one bad client can't kill the accept loop; closes the connection on exit.

#global

0/1 documented
1 undocumented
  • trampoline()builtin

#Structs

238 types
beagle.core/Function

A first-class function object.

  • pointer
  • name
  • arity
beagle.core/NEVER

Internal sentinel struct (workaround for id 0 activating the fast-path cache).

beagle.core/Struct

Runtime descriptor for a struct type: its name and numeric id.

  • name
  • id
beagle.core/Protocol

Runtime descriptor for a protocol, identified by name.

  • name
beagle.core/Thread

Holds a thread's closure and acts as a GC root while the thread runs.

  • closure
beagle.core/Atom

Thread-safe mutable reference cell holding a single value.

  • value
beagle.core/ArraySeq

Seq view over a raw Array, tracking the current index.

  • index
beagle.core/PersistentVectorSeq

Seq view over a PersistentVector, tracking the current index.

  • vec
  • index
beagle.core/Stdout

Default Writer that sends output to standard out.

beagle.core/StringBuffer

Mutable Writer that accumulates written output into a string.

beagle.ffi/Library

Handle to a dynamically loaded shared library; id is the runtime's internal library identifier.

  • id
beagle.ffi/Pointer

A raw C pointer split into low and high 32-bit halves (lo, hi).

  • lo
  • hi
beagle.ffi/Buffer

An off-heap byte buffer: ptr is the raw address, size its byte length. Registered with the GC finalizer so the backing allocation is freed when the Buffer becomes unreachable.

  • ptr
  • size
beagle.ffi/Cell

A typed one-element off-heap cell: ptr raw address, size byte length, ty the FFI element Type. GC-finalized like Buffer.

  • ptr
  • size
  • ty
beagle.ffi/TypedArray

A typed fixed-length off-heap array: ptr raw address, size total bytes, ty the element Type, length the element count. GC-finalized like Buffer.

  • ptr
  • size
  • ty
  • length
beagle.ffi/StructReturn

Holds a by-value C struct return of up to 16 bytes: low is the first 8 bytes, high the second 8 bytes.

  • low
  • high

beagle.async/TcpIoError

Sentinel value returned (not thrown) for a failed async TCP op, so tcp-result-or-throw can re-raise it in the caller's stack. Holds the error message.

beagle.async/AwaitRejected

Sentinel: the awaited future rejected; await re-raises error.

beagle.async/AwaitCancelled

Sentinel: the awaited future was cancelled; await throws.

beagle.async/SchedulerTask

A parked cooperative task: the PendingOp it waits on plus the continuation to resume when it completes.

  • resume
beagle.async/CooperativeHandler

Cooperative single-threaded async handler state: an atom of parked tasks plus the event-loop id.

beagle.async/BlockingAsyncHandler

Async handler that runs every operation synchronously on the current thread. Used for deterministic testing.

beagle.async/ImplicitAsyncHandler

The primary production async handler: threaded spawn/await combined with event-loop sleep and async file I/O.

beagle.fs/BlockingFsHandler

Synchronous (blocking) handler for the Fs effect. Its handle implementation executes each Fs operation immediately using the underlying beagle.io/beagle.core syscalls and resumes with the Result.

beagle.timer/Timer
  • Sleep
  • Now
beagle.timer/Timer.Sleep
  • ms

beagle.stream/StreamResult
  • Value
  • Done
  • Error
beagle.stream/StreamResult.Value
  • value
beagle.stream/StreamResult.Done
beagle.stream/StreamResult.Error
  • error
beagle.stream/Stream

Stream wrapper - provides functional combinators over a StreamSource

Never instantiate directly; use from-source() or combinator functions.

  • source
  • closed
beagle.stream/MapSource

StreamSource that applies a mapper function to each upstream value.

  • upstream
  • mapper
beagle.stream/FilterSource

StreamSource that yields only upstream values matching the predicate.

  • upstream
  • predicate
beagle.stream/TakeSource

StreamSource that yields at most a remaining number of upstream values.

  • upstream
  • remaining
beagle.stream/TakeWhileSource

StreamSource that yields upstream values while the predicate holds, then stops.

  • upstream
  • predicate
  • done
beagle.stream/SkipSource

StreamSource that discards the first to_skip upstream values, then passes through.

  • upstream
  • to_skip
beagle.stream/DropWhileSource

StreamSource that drops the leading prefix matching the predicate, then passes the rest through.

  • upstream
  • predicate
  • dropping
beagle.stream/FlatMapSource

StreamSource that maps each upstream value to a stream and flattens the results.

  • upstream
  • mapper
  • current_inner
beagle.stream/FileChunkSource

StreamSource that reads a file as raw byte chunks using async I/O.

  • path
  • chunk_size
  • file_handle
  • eof
beagle.stream/FileChunkSourceSync

StreamSource that reads a file as raw byte chunks using synchronous I/O.

  • path
  • chunk_size
  • file_handle
  • eof
beagle.stream/DirSource

StreamSource that lazily iterates directory entry names.

  • path
  • entries
  • index
  • loaded
beagle.stream/SplitOnSource

StreamSource that buffers bytes and yields messages split on a delimiter.

  • upstream
  • delimiter
  • buffer
beagle.stream/BySizeSource

StreamSource that buffers bytes and yields fixed-size chunks.

  • upstream
  • size
  • buffer
beagle.stream/GeneratorSource

StreamSource that pulls values from a generator function.

  • generator
  • stopped
beagle.stream/MergeSource

StreamSource that interleaves two upstream streams round-robin.

  • left
  • right
  • left_done
  • right_done
  • turn
beagle.stream/ZipSource

StreamSource that pairs values from two upstream streams.

  • left
  • right
beagle.stream/BufferedSource

StreamSource that batches upstream values into an internal buffer.

  • upstream
  • buffer_size
  • buffer
  • pos
beagle.stream/DistinctSource

StreamSource that yields each upstream value only the first time it is seen.

  • upstream
  • seen
beagle.stream/ScanSource

StreamSource that emits init then each running accumulator of a left fold.

  • upstream
  • accumulator
  • reducer
  • emitted_init
beagle.stream/CatchSource

StreamSource that replaces an upstream error with a default value.

  • upstream
  • default
beagle.stream/RetrySource

StreamSource that retries the upstream on error up to max_retries times.

  • upstream
  • max_retries
  • retries
beagle.stream/SocketChunkSource

StreamSource that reads a TCP socket as raw byte chunks.

  • socket
  • chunk_size
  • eof
beagle.repl-session/EvalRequest

A queued evaluation: the request id, the code string to eval, and result_atom where the resulting response list is published.

  • id
  • code
  • result_atom
beagle.repl-session/Session

One REPL connection's evaluation state: its id, the dedicated eval_thread, the message_queue of pending requests, the is_running busy flag, the cancel_token, the resume_stack of suspended exceptions, and ns, the session's pinned current namespace.

  • id
  • eval_thread
  • message_queue
  • is_running
  • cancel_token
  • resume_stack
  • ns
beagle.repl-main/MainEvalRequest

A queued eval request handed from a server thread to the main thread. result_atom starts null; the main thread sets it to an EvalResult once done.

  • id
  • code
  • result_atom
beagle.repl-main/EvalResult

The outcome of evaluating one request: captured output, the result value as a string, and an error message (null when evaluation succeeded).

  • output
  • value
  • error
beagle.spawn/Spawn
  • Spawn
  • SpawnWithToken
beagle.spawn/Spawn.Spawn
  • thunk
beagle.spawn/Spawn.SpawnWithToken
  • thunk
  • token
beagle.spawn/BlockingSpawnHandler

Spawn handler that runs tasks immediately and synchronously on the current thread, so spawned futures are already settled when returned.

beagle.spawn/ThreadedSpawnHandler

Spawn handler that runs each task on its own OS thread for parallel execution, returning a Future that settles when the thread finishes.

beagle.mutable-array/ExampleStruct

A trivial single-field struct used only by this module's demos/tests (allocate-array-and-return and main).

  • value
beagle.hash/W64

A 64-bit word emulated as two 32-bit halves (hi = upper 32 bits, lo = lower 32 bits).

  • hi
  • lo
beagle.url/Url

A parsed URL with fields scheme, host, port, path, query and fragment, as returned by parse-url. port is an Int or null; query/fragment are the raw strings after '?'/'#' or null when absent; path is "" when none is present.

  • scheme
  • host
  • port
  • path
  • query
  • fragment
beagle.http/Request

A parsed HTTP request: method, path, query, version, a lowercased-key headers map, and the body string.

  • method
  • path
  • query
  • version
  • headers
  • body
  • params
beagle.http/Response

An HTTP response to write (server) or parsed from a call (client): status code, reason phrase, a lowercased-key headers map, and the body string.

  • status
  • reason
  • headers
  • body
beagle.http/Route

A single router entry pairing a method + path with its handler.

  • method
  • path
  • handler
beagle.http/ConnBuf

A buffered wrapper over a raw socket connection, holding an atom of over-read leftover bytes carried across read calls.

  • conn
  • leftover
  • tls
beagle.template/Token

A lexer token produced by tokenize: kind is "literal", "expr", "raw-expr", or "tag"; text is the verbatim run or the inner expression/directive.

  • kind
  • text
beagle.template/TextNode

AST node for literal output: text is copied verbatim on render.

  • text
beagle.template/VarNode

AST node for an interpolation: path is the keyword lookup path; escape is true to HTML-escape the looked-up value.

  • path
  • escape
beagle.template/IfNode

AST node for {% if %}: path is the condition lookup; then-body and else-body are node vectors (else-body is empty when there is no {% else %}).

  • path
  • then-body
  • else-body
beagle.template/ForNode

AST node for {% for %}: var is the loop-binding keyword, path locates the vector to iterate, and body is the node vector rendered per element.

  • var
  • path
  • body
beagle.template/CompiledTemplate

A pre-parsed template produced by compile: nodes is the parsed AST node vector and size-hint seeds the output string-builder. Render it any number of times with render-compiled without re-tokenizing or re-parsing.

  • nodes
  • size-hint
beagle.ws/Frame

A parsed WebSocket frame: fin (final-fragment Bool), opcode (Int), masked (Bool: was the payload masked), payload (unmasked byte-faithful String).

  • fin
  • opcode
  • masked
  • payload
beagle.containers/Deque
  • front
  • back
beagle.containers/DefaultMap
  • default
  • table
beagle.containers/OrderedMap
  • order
  • table
beagle.json/Cursor

Mutable parse cursor: source string s, in-place byte index pos, and keyword-keys flag controlling whether object keys parse as keywords.

  • s
  • pos mut
  • keyword-keys
beagle.bigint/BigInt

Arbitrary-precision integer. sign is -1, 0, or 1; limbs is a least-significant-first Vec<Int> of base-10000 groups with no leading-zero limbs. Zero is canonical: sign 0 with an empty limbs vector.

  • sign
  • limbs
beagle.channel/Channel

Unbounded FIFO channel: an atom holding a persistent-vector queue plus an atom holding a closed flag (false until close! flips it true).

  • queue
  • closed
beagle.channel/_ChannelQueue
  • sendq
  • recvq
beagle.channel/Mutex

CAS-acquired lock: wraps an atom holding a bool (false = unlocked, true = locked).

  • locked
beagle.channel/Counter

Atomic integer counter: wraps an atom holding an int.

  • value
beagle.date/DateTime

A broken-down UTC calendar date-time: year, month (1-12), day (1-31), hour (0-23), minute (0-59), second (0-59). All fields are plain integers.

  • year
  • month
  • day
  • hour
  • minute
  • second
beagle.priorityqueue/PriorityQueue

A binary min-heap priority queue. items is a persistent vector holding the heap (children of index i at 2i+1/2i+2); compare is the ordering function (negative => first arg pops before second). Immutable: operations return a new PriorityQueue.

  • items
  • compare
beagle.semver/SemVer

A parsed Semantic Version. major/minor/patch are ints; prerelease and build are the raw strings after the -/+ markers (null when absent).

  • major
  • minor
  • patch
  • prerelease
  • build

#Enums

15 types
beagle.core/SystemError

Built-in error variants raised by the runtime; each carries a message and location.

  • StructError { message, location }
  • ParseError { message, location }
  • CompileError { message, location }
  • TypeError { message, location }
  • ArityError { message, location }
  • RuntimeError { message, location }
  • ThreadError { message, location }
  • FFIError { message, location }
  • FunctionError { message, location }
  • FieldError { message, location }
  • IndexError { message, location }
  • MutabilityError { message, location }
  • JsonError { message, location }
  • AssertError { message, location }
  • IOError { message, location }
  • AllocationError { message, location }
  • ArgumentError { message, location }
  • InvalidArgument { message, location }
  • StringError { message, location }
  • RegexError { message, location }
  • EncodingError { message, location }
  • CopyError { message, location }
beagle.core/DiagnosticSeverity

Severity levels for compiler diagnostics: error, warning, info, hint.

  • error
  • warning
  • info
  • hint
beagle.core/Error

Canonical error type for async and I/O operations; pattern-match to handle each case.

  • NotFound { path }
  • PermissionDenied { path }
  • AlreadyExists { path }
  • IsDirectory { path }
  • NotDirectory { path }
  • Timeout { operation }
  • Cancelled
  • IO { message }
  • Other { code, message }
beagle.core/Result

Canonical Ok/Err result type used across async and I/O operations.

  • Ok { value }
  • Err { error }
beagle.ffi/Type

Enumeration of FFI value types: the fixed-width integers (U8..U64, I8..I64), floats (F32/F64), Pointer/MutablePointer, String, Void, and a nested Structure { types } for aggregate layouts.

  • U8
  • U16
  • U32
  • U64
  • I8
  • I16
  • I32
  • I64
  • F32
  • F64
  • Pointer
  • MutablePointer
  • String
  • Void
  • Structure { types }
beagle.async/FutureState

A future's lifecycle state: Pending, Running, Resolved (with value), Rejected (with error), or Cancelled.

  • Pending
  • Running
  • Resolved { value }
  • Rejected { error }
  • Cancelled
beagle.async/RaceResult

Result of await-first: Ok with the winning value and its index, or AllFailed with the collected errors.

  • Ok { value, index }
  • AllFailed { errors }
beagle.async/TimeoutResult

Result of a timed await: Ok with the value, or TimedOut if the deadline passed.

  • Ok { value }
  • TimedOut
beagle.async/IOAction

Describes a TCP I/O operation for the async handler to perform (listen, connect, accept, read, write, close).

  • TcpListen { host, port }
  • TcpConnect { host, port }
  • TcpAccept { listener }
  • TcpRead { socket, n }
  • TcpWrite { socket, data }
  • TcpClose { socket }
  • TcpCloseListener { listener }
beagle.async/PendingOp

A pending cooperative-scheduler operation: TCP I/O, file I/O, a timer, a spawned-task wait, or a new task to start.

  • TcpIO { op_id, loop_id }
  • FileIO { handle, loop_id }
  • Timer { marker, loop_id }
  • SpawnedTask { future }
  • NewTask { thunk }
beagle.async/Async

The Async effect: futures (await/cancel), generic TCP I/O, file and directory operations, low-level file handles, sleep, and spawning.

  • Await { future }
  • AwaitAll { futures }
  • AwaitFirst { futures }
  • Cancel { future }
  • IO { action }
  • ReadFile { path }
  • WriteFile { path, content }
  • AppendFile { path, content }
  • DeleteFile { path }
  • FileExists { path }
  • RenameFile { old_path, new_path }
  • CopyFile { src_path, dest_path }
  • ReadDir { path }
  • CreateDir { path }
  • CreateDirAll { path }
  • RemoveDir { path }
  • RemoveDirAll { path }
  • FileSize { path }
  • IsDirectory { path }
  • IsFile { path }
  • Open { path, mode }
  • Close { file }
  • Read { file, n }
  • Write { file, content }
  • ReadLine { file }
  • Flush { file }
  • Sleep { ms }
  • Spawn { thunk }
  • SpawnWithToken { thunk, token }
beagle.fs/Fs

The filesystem effect. Each variant is a distinct file or directory operation that is dispatched to whatever Handler(Fs) is installed. Performed indirectly by the wrapper functions below (read-file, etc.).

  • ReadFile { path }
  • WriteFile { path, content }
  • AppendFile { path, content }
  • DeleteFile { path }
  • FileExists { path }
  • FileSize { path }
  • IsFile { path }
  • RenameFile { old_path, new_path }
  • CopyFile { src_path, dest_path }
  • ReadDir { path }
  • CreateDir { path }
  • CreateDirAll { path }
  • RemoveDir { path }
  • RemoveDirAll { path }
  • IsDirectory { path }
  • Open { path, mode }
  • Close { file }
  • Read { file, n }
  • Write { file, content }
  • ReadLine { file }
  • Flush { file }
beagle.timer/Timer
  • Sleep { ms }
  • Now
beagle.stream/StreamResult

Stream result - outcome of pulling the next value

  • Value { value }
  • Done
  • Error { error }
beagle.spawn/Spawn

Effect operations for spawning tasks. Spawn carries a thunk to run; SpawnWithToken also carries a cancellation token. Performed via the spawn/spawn-with-token helpers and interpreted by an installed handler.

  • Spawn { thunk }
  • SpawnWithToken { thunk, token }
